soc_dashboard_update.title: Eveus: update SOC cards and automations to use native SOC entities
soc_dashboard_update.description:
The Eveus integration now creates SOC input entities automatically for your charger.

These native entities replace the old manually created `input_number.ev_*` helpers. Existing values are copied automatically, so you do not need to enter them again.

Update any dashboard cards, automations, scripts, or templates that still use the old helper entities.

Replace this old prefix:

`input_number.ev_`

with this new Eveus integration prefix:

`number.eveus_ev_charger_`

This updates all four SOC inputs:

`input_number.ev_initial_soc` → `number.eveus_ev_charger_initial_soc`

`input_number.ev_target_soc` → `number.eveus_ev_charger_target_soc`

`input_number.ev_battery_capacity` → `number.eveus_ev_charger_battery_capacity`

`input_number.ev_soc_correction` → `number.eveus_ev_charger_soc_correction`

The Eveus integration does not delete or modify your old `input_number.ev_*` helpers.

After you update all dashboards, automations, scripts, and templates to the new `number.eveus_ev_charger_*` entities, you can delete the old helpers if you no longer need them.

ocpp_enabled.title: Eveus: OCPP is enabled, Home Assistant control may be limited
ocpp_enabled.description:
OCPP is currently enabled on your Eveus charger.

While OCPP is enabled, the charger may follow commands from the OCPP server or the Grizzl-E Connect mobile app instead of Home Assistant. Some Home Assistant controls may not apply or may be overwritten.

This can affect:

- Charging current
- Charge limits
- Charging schedule

To restore full Home Assistant control, turn off the **Connect to OCPP** switch for your Eveus charger.

The Eveus integration will clear this warning automatically after OCPP is turned off.

battery_low.title: Eveus: charger battery is low, replace it soon
battery_low.description:
The CR2032 coin-cell battery inside your Eveus charger is low.

While it is low, some charger functions may be limited.

What to do:

- Replace it with a fresh, good-quality CR2032 cell.

The Eveus integration will clear this warning automatically once a new battery reads a healthy voltage.

safety_ground_missing.title: Eveus: ground connection is missing, stop charging
safety_ground_missing.description:
The charger reports no ground connection.

This is a physical grounding fault, reported independently of the **Ground Protection** switch, so you may also see a separate warning if that protection is disabled.

Charging without a working ground can be dangerous.

What to do:

- Stop charging and have the grounding checked by a qualified electrician.
- Keep the **Ground Protection** switch enabled unless an electrician confirms a reason not to.

The Eveus integration clears this warning automatically once the ground connection is stable.

safety_ground_control_disabled.title: Eveus: ground protection is disabled
safety_ground_control_disabled.description:
Ground protection is disabled on the charger.

The charger may continue charging when the ground connection is missing. If ground is also missing, Eveus will show a separate **Ground connection is missing** repair issue.

What to do:

- Turn on the **Ground Protection** switch for your Eveus charger.

If this is intentional, press Ignore. The Eveus integration will clear this warning automatically after ground protection is enabled.

safety_leakage_detected.title: Eveus: current leakage detected, stop charging
safety_leakage_detected.description:
The charger reports current leakage.

Continuing to charge may be dangerous.

What to do:

- Stop charging and contact the charger manufacturer's support; the vehicle, cable, or installation may need checking.

This warning remains until the leakage stops and you press Ignore.

safety_relay_fault.title: Eveus: relay fault detected, stop charging
safety_relay_fault.description:
The charger reports a relay fault.

The relay may not switch charging power safely.

What to do:

- Stop using the charger and contact the charger manufacturer's support.

This warning remains until the fault clears and you press Ignore.

safety_box_overheat.title: Eveus: charger temperature is too high, stop charging
safety_box_overheat.description:
The charger reports an overheat fault or a sustained body temperature of at least 80 °C.

Home Assistant warns before the charger automatically stops charging at 85 °C.

What to do:

- Stop charging and let the charger cool.
- Check ventilation and contact the charger manufacturer's support if this repeats.

This warning remains until the temperature returns to normal and you press Ignore.

safety_plug_overheat.title: Eveus: plug temperature is too high, stop charging
safety_plug_overheat.description:
The charger reports an overheat fault or a sustained plug temperature of at least 80 °C.

Home Assistant warns before the charger automatically stops charging at 85 °C.

What to do:

- Stop charging and let the plug cool.
- Once safe, contact the charger manufacturer's support to have the plug, socket, and cable checked.

This warning remains until the temperature returns to normal and you press Ignore.

safety_pilot_fault.title: Eveus: control pilot fault detected, stop charging
safety_pilot_fault.description:
The charger reports a control pilot fault and cannot confirm a safe charging connection.

What to do:

- Stop charging and contact the charger manufacturer's support; the cable or vehicle connection may be at fault.

This warning remains until the fault clears and you press Ignore.

safety_low_voltage.title: Eveus: supply voltage is too low, pause charging
safety_low_voltage.description:
The charger reports that supply voltage is too low.

What to do:

- Pause charging until voltage is stable.
- If this repeats, have the electrical supply checked.

This warning remains until the fault clears and you press Ignore.

safety_diode_fault.title: Eveus: vehicle diode fault detected, stop charging
safety_diode_fault.description:
The charger reports a vehicle diode fault.

What to do:

- Stop charging and contact the charger manufacturer's support; the vehicle or charging cable may be at fault.

This warning remains until the fault clears and you press Ignore.

safety_overcurrent.title: Eveus: charging current is too high, stop charging
safety_overcurrent.description:
The charger reports charging current above its safe limit.

What to do:

- Stop charging and contact the charger manufacturer's support; the charger, cable, or installation may need checking.

This warning remains until the fault clears and you press Ignore.

safety_interface_timeout.title: Eveus: internal communication fault detected
safety_interface_timeout.description:
The charger reports an internal communication timeout.

What to do:

- Stop charging.
- Restart the charger only if it is safe. Contact the charger manufacturer's support if this repeats.

This warning remains until the fault clears and you press Ignore.

safety_software_failure.title: Eveus: internal software fault detected
safety_software_failure.description:
The charger reports an internal software failure.

What to do:

- Stop charging.
- Restart the charger only if it is safe. Contact the charger manufacturer's support if this repeats.

This warning remains until the fault clears and you press Ignore.

safety_gfci_test_failure.title: Eveus: ground-fault protection test failed
safety_gfci_test_failure.description:
The charger reports that its ground-fault protection self-test failed.

Charging may not be protected from current leakage.

What to do:

- Stop using the charger and contact the charger manufacturer's support before charging again.

This warning remains until the fault clears and you press Ignore.

safety_high_voltage.title: Eveus: supply voltage is too high, stop charging
safety_high_voltage.description:
The charger reports that supply voltage is too high.

What to do:

- Stop charging until voltage is stable.
- If this repeats, have the electrical supply checked.

This warning remains until the fault clears and you press Ignore.

clock_drift.title: Eveus: charger clock is off by more than 10 minutes
clock_drift.description:
The clock inside your Eveus charger differs from Home Assistant by more than 10 minutes.

While the clocks disagree, charging schedules and tariff-based costs may apply at the wrong times.

What to do:

- Check that the **Time Zone** select for your Eveus charger matches your local time zone.
- Press the **Sync Time** button for your Eveus charger to set its clock from Home Assistant.

The Eveus integration will clear this warning automatically once the clocks agree again.
